name: direct_prism_shell_boundary_layer_v0_1
description: Custom body-fitted prism-shell boundary-layer component. The standalone shell is not a complete external-flow mesh, but the shell-plus-Gmsh exact-merge hybrid path now produces complete OpenFOAM plumbing meshes.

validated_scope.solver_case_status: standalone shell strict checkMesh only; hybrid exact-merge farfield cases pass strict checkMesh and potentialFoam

five_variant_result.note: fcv01_long_glider required alternate remesh feature angle 45 deg; the original fd25 shell failed only on skewness.

known_limitations: The outer patch is an offset aircraft shell, not a proper farfield box/tunnel.
The complete hybrid mesh requires a Gmsh tetrahedral external volume and the custom exact interface merge utility.
OpenFOAM mergeMeshes plus stitchMesh did not produce a valid combined shell-plus-Gmsh mesh; use custom exact merge instead.
Coarser decimated direct-prism shells can introduce localized degenerate cells near wingtip/trailing-edge areas.
This preset is solver-plumbing ready but not scoring-CFD ready; aircraft wall treatment, y+ policy, and validated force coefficients are still pending.

notes: The hybrid path uses watertight conditioned aircraft surfaces, not raw exporter STLs.
A no-optimizer Gmsh outer-volume run produced an isolated high-aspect-cell failure on fcv04; default,Netgen fixed it.
The current prism shell has three cumulative offsets: 0.2 mm, 0.5 mm, and 1.0 mm.

next_required_work: Promote the hybrid exact-merge driver into the optimizer mesher backend contract.
Add automated localization for direct-shell bad cells and reject decimated surfaces that introduce local prism degeneracy.
Define the first bounded no-slip OpenFOAM scoring experiment on the hybrid meshes without treating the coefficients as validated scoring output.
